title: Add online latent tyre-state features from a lightweight state-space filter
summary: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent stochastic state with pit-stop resets and shows better rolling-origin prediction than an AR(1) benchmark.
rationale: Recent F1 research models tyre degradation as a latent stochastic state with pit-stop resets and shows better rolling-origin prediction than an AR(1) benchmark. That fits this task well because `PitNextLap` should depend on hidden tyre condition, not only observed `TyreLife` and cumulative degradation.
implementation_hint: Per driver-race-stint, fit or approximate a simple online Kalman/state-space filter using only past laps to estimate latent pace, latent degradation slope, slope acceleration, reset-adjusted warmup state, and posterior uncertainty. Feed only filtered states available up to the current row into XGB/CatBoost.
expected_effect: Should add a cleaner degradation signal than hand-built rolling deltas, especially when observed lap times are noisy from traffic or driver errors.
risk: Too-rich filtering can leak future information if smoothed instead of filtered states are used. A weak approximate filter may collapse to features you already have.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
